author: Dr. James Wilson
hpi: Mr. Morrison is a 72-year-old male with HFrEF (EF 30-35%, diagnosed 2019), persistent A-fib (off anticoagulation since GI bleed September 2023), CKD 3b, Type 2 DM, and a complex medication history who presents for cardiology follow-up. He was started on empagliflozin 10mg daily on May 8, 2025 — now approximately 2 weeks into therapy. This addition completes his quadruple GDMT for HFrEF (carvedilol 25mg BID, sacubitril/valsartan 97/103mg BID, spironolactone 25mg daily, empagliflozin 10mg daily). Labs drawn 2 weeks after empagliflozin start show Cr 2.05 (baseline 1.93, representing an expected initial hemodynamic dip in eGFR with SGLT2i — not structural), K 4.6 (actually improved, likely from natriuretic effect), and glucose levels improved per home monitoring. He reports he feels about the same as before starting — no improvement in dyspnea yet (which is expected at 2 weeks, as SGLT2i benefits accrue over weeks to months). He denies any new urinary symptoms beyond a mild increase in daytime frequency. No genital itching, discharge, or signs of infection. No nausea, vomiting, or abdominal pain that could suggest ketoacidosis. He reports stable exertional dyspnea — can walk 1-2 blocks before needing to rest. He has occasional orthopnea (2-pillow night, unchanged). No PND episodes. His weight has remained stable at approximately 97.5 kg. He continues to decline ICD implantation for primary prevention despite meeting criteria — wants more time to discuss with family.

reviewOfSystems.Constitutional: Reports chronic fatigue, unchanged. No fever, chills, or significant weight change.
reviewOfSystems.Cardiovascular: Stable exertional dyspnea (1-2 blocks). Occasional 2-pillow orthopnea, unchanged. No PND, chest pain, or palpitations. No syncope or presyncope.
reviewOfSystems.Respiratory: Mild exertional dyspnea at baseline. No cough, hemoptysis, or wheezing.
reviewOfSystems.GI: No nausea, vomiting, abdominal pain, or change in bowel habits. No melena or hematochezia. Appetite fair.
reviewOfSystems.GU: Mild increase in daytime urinary frequency since empagliflozin (expected). Nocturia x2 (unchanged). No dysuria, hematuria, or genital symptoms.
reviewOfSystems.Neurologic: Chronic bilateral foot numbness and tingling. No dizziness, syncope, or new weakness.
reviewOfSystems.Endocrine: No symptoms suggestive of DKA. No excessive thirst. Home glucoses improved.
reviewOfSystems.Psychiatric: Patient less talkative than usual. Wife not present today.

physicalExam.General: Mildly fatigued-appearing overweight male in no acute distress. Conversational but less animated than prior visits.
physicalExam.HEENT: Normocephalic, atraumatic. PERRL. Oropharynx clear. No JVD appreciated at 45 degrees.
physicalExam.Cardiovascular: Irregularly irregular rhythm (A-fib), rate 67. S1 normal, S2 physiologically split. No S3 gallop (improved — S3 had been intermittently present in past). Soft 2/6 holosystolic murmur at apex radiating to left axilla (known moderate MR). No diastolic murmurs. No rubs. JVP estimated 7 cm H2O (normal). Carotid upstrokes normal, no bruits.
physicalExam.Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No crackles (improved from mild bibasilar crackles at last visit). No wheezes. Good bilateral air entry.
physicalExam.Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatojugular reflux. No hepatomegaly.
physicalExam.Extremities: 1+ bilateral pedal edema (mild, stable). Warm, well-perfused. Capillary refill <2 seconds.
physicalExam.Neurologic: Alert and oriented x3. Decreased sensation bilateral feet. Motor intact.

assessment.diagnosis: Heart Failure with Reduced EF, NYHA Class II, on optimized GDMT
assessment.notes: Now on quadruple therapy: BB (carvedilol 25mg BID — target dose), ARNI (sacubitril/valsartan 97/103mg BID — target dose), MRA (spironolactone 25mg — target dose), SGLT2i (empagliflozin 10mg daily — newly added). EF 30-35% on most recent echo (September 2020). Clinically stable NYHA II. No S3 today (possible early improvement). Lungs clear. Volume status appears well-compensated. ICD discussion ongoing — patient meets criteria (EF ≤35%, NYHA II-III, on optimal GDMT >3 months) but continues to defer.

Cr 2.05, representing expected hemodynamic dip in eGFR (approximately 6% decline). This is a known pharmacological effect of SGLT2 inhibitors related to afferent arteriolar vasoconstriction and is NOT indicative of structural kidney injury. K 4.6 (improved from 4.7). Glucose improved. No adverse effects (no genital infections, no UTI, no DKA symptoms). Safe to continue.

Rate controlled at 67. Not on anticoagulation — permanent decision post-GI hemorrhage September 2023. CHA2DS2-VASc 5. On aspirin 81mg. TEE September 2024 showed no LAA thrombus. Watchman device discussed previously — patient not interested.

assessment.diagnosis: CKD Stage 3b with expected SGLT2i-related eGFR dip
assessment.notes: Cr 2.05 (baseline 1.93). Expected transient rise. Will recheck in 4-6 weeks. If Cr rises >30% from baseline, would need to reassess.

Meets Class I indication: EF ≤35%, NYHA II, on GDMT >3 months, life expectancy >1 year. Patient has been deferring since October 2022. Discussed again today — understands benefits for primary prevention of sudden cardiac death. Wants to discuss with wife and children. Will revisit at next visit.

plan.action: Quadruple therapy now complete. Continue all at current doses. No dose adjustments needed. Monitor for continued clinical improvement — SGLT2i benefits typically manifest over 4-12 weeks. Continue daily weights, 2g sodium diet, 1.5L fluid restriction. Furosemide 60mg AM/40mg PM unchanged.

2-week labs reassuring — expected Cr rise, no hyperkalemia, no adverse effects. Continue empagliflozin 10mg daily. Recheck BMP in 4 weeks (late June). If Cr stabilizes or improves, continue. If Cr rises >30% from baseline (>2.5), hold empagliflozin and investigate. Counseled patient on sick-day rules: hold empagliflozin if acutely ill, fasting, or undergoing surgery.
Rate well-controlled on carvedilol. Continue aspirin 81mg. No anticoagulation change.

Patient informed of continued indication. Provided ICD patient education handout. Encouraged discussion with family. Will revisit at next cardiology appointment in 3 months. If patient agrees, will refer to electrophysiology for device implantation.

Cardiology in 3 months (August-September 2024). Labs before next visit: BMP in 4 weeks, then BMP/CBC/BNP before next appointment. TEE to be scheduled for comprehensive reassessment of cardiac structure and function.

attestation:
I personally saw and examined the patient. I reviewed the 2-week post-empagliflozin laboratory results. I agree with the documented findings and plan.

Electronically signed by: Dr. James Wilson, MD
Date: 5/23/2024
